description: Deleting the entire generated consumer crate and resyncing from the ontology reproduces it byte-identically, and the rebuilt crate builds and its generated tests/falsifiers all pass -- but only after a real, pack-level defect (found this pass) was fixed in the scratch copy. The as-shipped pack fails at the first step (cargo build) with a hard compile error, so this cannot be marked a flat ALIVE.

critical_finding_FRONTMATTER_LEAK.summary:
Every one of the 14 templates/**/*.tmpl files in the as-shipped pack begins with a `---
to: ...
force: true
sparql:
  results: |
    ...
---` frontmatter header. gpacks/mfw-pcp-level5/ggen.toml uses ggen's DECLARATIVE-RULES ggen.toml schema (non-empty [[generation.rules]], each rule already carrying its own separate query={file=...}/template={file=...}/output_file/mode). Per crates/ggen-engine/src/generation_rules.rs's own module doc (lines 1-18), that per-template frontmatter convention belongs exclusively to ggen's OTHER, mutually-exclusive 'frontmatter' ggen.toml schema (crate::config::GgenConfig + crate::template::Frontmatter). The declarative-rules pipeline never parses or strips it -- it treats the entire .tmpl file as the raw Tera template body. Result: every generated output file (Cargo.toml, all *.rs, README.md, PROVENANCE.ttl, ci.yml, RELEASE_STANDING.json, verify.sh) carries the literal, unrendered 8-10 line YAML header prepended to otherwise-correct content.
critical_finding_FRONTMATTER_LEAK.reproduction: rm -rf .ggen-v2 .ggen consumer; ggen sync run --format json  (against the UNMODIFIED, as-shipped pack) -> exit 0, reports 14 files written, receipt 'Admitted'/'Pass' for all of them -- but every written file's first line is literally '---'.
critical_finding_FRONTMATTER_LEAK.compile_proof: cd consumer/mfw-pcp-generated && cargo build  ->  error: key with no value, expected `=`  --> Cargo.toml:1:4  |  1 | ---  |    ^   (the leaked frontmatter delimiter is invalid TOML; the generated consumer crate cannot compile at all)
critical_finding_FRONTMATTER_LEAK.classification: Decorative Completion (per .claude/rules/coding-agent-mistakes.md in the ggen repo, class 1): `ggen sync run` exits 0 and the receipt says 'Pass', but no durable, USABLE state was produced -- the generated crate is not valid Rust/TOML.
critical_finding_FRONTMATTER_LEAK.relevance_beyond_scope: This also directly triggers cap05's own stated falsifier ('generated proof does not compile or fails'), which is currently marked PARTIAL_ALIVE in the ledger and was out of this pass's assigned scope (cap03/04/09/11/12 only). Flagging for whoever next revisits cap05/06/07/08/10 -- not changed by this pass.
critical_finding_FRONTMATTER_LEAK.fix_applied_scratch_copy_only: Mechanically stripped the frontmatter header (everything from the first '---' line through the matching second '---' line) from all 14 templates/**/*.tmpl files in /private/tmp/mfw-pcp-l5-exec only. The header's 'to'/'force'/'sparql.results' keys are redundant under the declarative-rules schema (already supplied by ggen.toml's [[generation.rules]] output_file/mode/query fields), so removing them changes nothing semantically -- it only removes dead text that was leaking into output.
critical_finding_FRONTMATTER_LEAK.source_pack_untouched: The verified source at .../gpacks/mfw-pcp-level5/templates/src/certificates.rs.tmpl (and all 13 siblings) still begins with '---' -- confirmed by direct read after this pass. This defect and its fix are NOT applied to the source; a maintainer must apply the equivalent fix (or an upstream ggen-engine feature to strip per-template frontmatter under the declarative-rules schema) before the source pack can honestly claim cap05/cap11/cap12.

commands: rm -rf consumer                          # delete the entire generated consumer crate
ggen sync run --format json               # resync from ontology.ttl (reverted to its original, unmodified state)
find <14 canonical output paths> | sort | xargs shasum -a 256 | shasum -a 256   # compare to pre-deletion baseline
bash scripts/verify.sh   # generated release-verification ladder (separate, adjacent finding below)

result.verify_sh_failure_detail: Fails at the first step, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, because the Tera-rendered Rust source is not rustfmt-canonical (long single-line struct literals, etc.). This is a separate, real, formatting-only defect in the templates -- not a correctness defect (cargo test alone is 6/6 green) and not what this capability's own falsifier text ("deleted consumer cannot be rebuilt and verified", read narrowly as tests/falsifiers passing) is asking about, but reported here for completeness since scripts/verify.sh is the pack's own designated verifier ladder (cap10's artifact, out of this pass's scope).

falsifier_checked: deleted consumer cannot be rebuilt and verified
note: PARTIAL_ALIVE, not ALIVE: the underlying delete-resync-build-test cycle genuinely works and is byte-identical/reproducible, but only once the FRONTMATTER_LEAK defect above is fixed. As shipped, the pack fails at the very first cargo build.
